verdict_reasoning: HSPA5 (BiP/GRP78) is canonically an ER-luminal chaperone with an ER-retention signal and no baseline surface localization. However, BiP is well-documented as a tumor-associated antigen displayed on the outer leaflet of cancer cells, and CAR-T and bispecific antibody programs specifically target this cancer-cell ecto-pool. This non-baseline surface display qualifies as cell_state_induced via oncogenic transformation. The protein body does reach the outer face, but only under malignant stress, not under baseline conditions.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Exact molecular mechanism of stress-induced BiP relocalization from ER lumen to plasma membrane outer leaflet in cancer cells is not fully resolved in trained knowledge; however, surface accessibility in cancer is documented and clinically actionable, so verdict is secure.
